mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the trigger activates, invalidating the predictive power of the unemployment shock.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive return differential over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with artificially low policy rates creates a negative real yield environment that triggers central bank tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows or payroll growth shows no correlation with quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with weak consumption signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and employment indicates synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or flat return distribution with a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over 5-day horizons in mid-cycle environments.
